title: US CPI cools to 2.4% in Jan as markets reprice Fed June cut path
content: US headline inflation slowed to 2.4% annually in January, down from 2.7% in December, according to BLS data released on 13 February 2026. Core CPI rose 0.3% monthly and 2.5% annually, meeting analyst expectations and confirming a gradual disinflationary trend despite previous concerns over 'sticky' services. While the data was slightly lower than consensus, the Fed is widely expected to maintain its 3.50%-3.75% pause at the March meeting to ensure target convergence. Markets reacted with a moderate drop in UST yields, while Fed Funds futures now price in a higher probability of the first rate cut in June. Equities traded with a slight positive bias as the threat of an immediate inflation flare-up receded, though the DXY remained resilient near 96.50.

title: ECB launches permanent Global Euro Repo Facility to bolster currency role
content: ECB President Christine Lagarde announced on 14 February 2026 that the euro liquidity backstop (EUREP) will become a permanent, globally accessible facility from Q3 2026. Speaking at the Munich Security Conference, Lagarde stated the move aims to prevent 'fire sales' of euro-denominated assets during global funding stress and to strengthen the euro’s international status. The facility will allow non-eurozone central banks to borrow up to €50 billion each against high-quality collateral. This structural shift follows a period of heightened market volatility and unpredictable US trade policy. Analysts view the move as a strategic effort to challenge USD dominance and provide a safety net for global holders of euro debt, which saw Bund and OAT spreads tighten slightly on the news.

title: UNCTAD report warns of major shifts in global trade as tariffs hit exports
content: A UNCTAD Global Trade Update released on 12 February 2026 detailed a radical redrawing of the competitive map due to uneven US tariff increases. The report highlighted significant price distortions, noting that South African wine exports became 17% more expensive relative to competitors, while Italian rice imports saw a 12% improvement in positioning. These policy-driven shifts are forcing a massive reconfiguration of global value chains and supplier diversification. Markets in the EU and APAC underperformed this week as manufacturing sectors faced increased trade uncertainty and rising input costs. Meanwhile, the IMF's revised 2026 global growth forecast of 3.3% remains overshadowed by downside risks from escalating protectionism, which continues to weigh on investor sentiment across commodity-dependent emerging markets.
